sources_primary: Aker BP Annual Statement of Reserves 2024 (PDF, akerbp.com)
Oil & Gas Journal - Skarv Crude Assay (BP)
Offshore Technology / NS Energy field profiles
SLB case study for Ivar Aasen
BP / Aker BP crude blend assays

fields_with_data.formation: Late Triassic to Early Cretaceous; aeolian, alluvial, shallow marine sandstones, conglomerates, weathered granitic basement (segments Luno, Jorvik, Tellus)
fields_with_data.api_source: Aker BP ASOR 2024 describes 'light and slightly undersaturated oil'; Edvard Grieg blend reported ~36-37 API in various market sources
fields_with_data.notes: Reservoir at ~1850-1900 m TVD MSL, in-situ temps 76-78C from microbial reservoir paper. ASOR 2024 mentions 'light and slightly undersaturated oil'. Sodir reports 97.9 MSm3 OOIP, 54.37 MSm3 recoverable -> RF ~55.5%. Reserves reduced in 2024 due to steeper-than-expected decline after produced water breakthrough.

fields_with_data.formation: Late Triassic to Late Jurassic; fluvial and shallow marine sandstones in Skagerrak, Sleipner and Hugin Formations; Triassic underlying
fields_with_data.api_source: ASOR 2024 - Ivar Aasen described as saturated oil with two gas caps; West Cable undersaturated; Sodir field metadata indicates moderate gravity crude
fields_with_data.notes: Two accumulations: Ivar Aasen (saturated, 2 gas caps) and West Cable (undersaturated). ASOR notes that formation pressure is very close to saturation pressure. 24.54 MSm3 recoverable oil / 46.00 MSm3 OOIP -> RF ~53.3%. Complex/heterogeneous reservoir. CoP expected end 2041.

fields_with_data.formation: Palaeocene Heimdal Formation (turbidite submarine fan sandstones); Hermod Formation injectites (Viper, Kobra)
fields_with_data.api_source: BP crude assay: 33.9 API (some sources cite 32.5 medium). Oil density 0.855 g/cc converts to ~34 API
fields_with_data.drive: Bottom aquifer drive; some gas cap expansion; depletion
fields_with_data.notes: Multi-field hub: Kameleon, Boa, Kneler, Viper, Kobra, Kobra East, Gekko, East Kameleon. Heimdal sandstones excellent quality. Reservoir depth 2100-2200m. Oil density 0.855g/cc, gas gravity 0.690g/cc, GOR ~80 m3/m3 (Heimdal Fm typical). Crude assay: API 33.9, low sulfur. KEG (Kobra East/Gekko) on stream 2023.

fields_with_data.formation: Early Eocene Hermod Formation injectite sandstones in Sele/Balder/Hordaland shales (massive injectite complex)
fields_with_data.api_source: Inferred similar to Alvheim/Heimdal area (~34 API). Volund crude blended with Alvheim FPSO production
fields_with_data.notes: Massive injectite complex with Darcy-quality sands. OWC at 1995 m TVD MSL, GOC at 1891 m TVD MSL. Original 100m oil column. Reservoir 2011-2018 m TVD MSL. 6 producers + 1 injector tied back to Alvheim FPSO.

fields_with_data.formation: Palaeocene Heimdal Formation turbidite sandstone (3 zones: R1 clean, R2 muddy baffle, R3 clean)
fields_with_data.api_source: Heimdal area analog. Undersaturated oil from ASOR. Vilje gas marketed via Alvheim PL203 commercial agreement
fields_with_data.drive: Natural water drive from regional Heimdal aquifer
fields_with_data.notes: Vilje Main + Vilje South. 56m net sand. Reservoir at 2135m TVD MSL. Heimdal aquifer depleted ~18 bar prior to Vilje first oil due to regional production history (Heimdal/Frigg). Undersaturated oil.

fields_with_data.formation: Palaeocene/Eocene Hermod Sandstone Formation (encased in Sele shales); Frosk reservoir is Intra Hordaland injectite
fields_with_data.api_source: Frosk biodegraded to relatively low quality (per ASOR); Bøyla main flagged as 'undersaturated oil at normal pressure' - estimate 30-33 API
fields_with_data.drive: Bøyla: water injection; Frosk: aquifer pressure support
fields_with_data.notes: Bøyla OWC at 2071m TVD MSL. Frosk reservoir at 1800m TVD MSL with GOC 1786m and OWC 1861m. Frosk oil is biodegraded to 'relatively low quality'. Frosk Attic infill approved 2024.

fields_with_data.formation: Eocene Upper Balder and Frigg Formation sandstones (deep marine turbidite, Frigg submarine fans)
fields_with_data.api_source: Heimdal/Frigg area analog with thin gas cap above oil
fields_with_data.notes: Crest at 2097m TVD MSL. NTG ~92%, porosity ~31%, perm ~1 Darcy. Reservoir at discovery (2010) was depleted 8% (~18 bar) below hydrostatic due to regional Frigg production. By 2019 pressure had risen ~3 bar showing aquifer support. Single bilateral producer tied to Vilje template.

fields_with_data.formation: Upper Cretaceous Tor and Hod Formations (chalk)
fields_with_data.api_source: Multiple sources: 'paraffinic 36 API oil' (Valhall Industriminne, NS Energy, Aker BP Production)
fields_with_data.drive: Pressure depletion + compaction drive; water injection from 2004; gas lift in most wells
fields_with_data.notes: Initial reservoir pressure ~450 bar (6535 psi) at 2500m. Currently depleted significantly; pressure below bubble point in upper Tor since 1988. Tor porosity up to 50% with matrix perm 1-10 mD, naturally fractured. Hod porosity 30-38% with perm 0.1-1 mD. RF 31% pre-injection, 38% with water injection. >1 bn boe produced (3x original PDO estimate). 9 reservoir regions. CoP 2049.

fields_with_data.formation: Lower Palaeocene Ekofisk Formation + Upper Cretaceous Tor and Hod Formations (chalk)
fields_with_data.api_source: Same fluid system as Valhall - paraffinic 36 API oil
fields_with_data.notes: 3 structures: Hod West, Hod East, Hod Saddle. Redeveloped 2021 with new Hod B unmanned wellhead platform (12 slots). Hod A decommissioned 2012. Hod Saddle produced via 3 wells from Valhall South Flank. CoP 2049 (same as Valhall).

fields_with_data.formation: Upper Jurassic Ula Formation sandstone (shallow marine); Triassic underlying
fields_with_data.api_source: Wikipedia / NS Energy: 'oil gravity 40 API'
fields_with_data.drive: Initially pressure depletion, then water injection + WAG (water alternating gas) from 1998
fields_with_data.notes: Net thickness 128m, porosity 14-28%, permeability mD to 2D, Sw 5-50%. Oil down to 3532m. 49 wells drilled, 8 producing + 4 injecting. Gas reinjected for IOR. Hub for Oda, Tambar, Blane. CoP planned 2028.

fields_with_data.formation: Upper Jurassic Ula Formation sandstone (shallow marine)
fields_with_data.api_source: Same Ula Formation fluid system as Ula field - ~40 API
fields_with_data.notes: Reservoir 4100-4200m. Generally very good quality. Cyclic production due to low reservoir pressure. CoP planned 2028. Tied to Ula for processing.

fields_with_data.formation: Jurassic Garn (high quality), Ile, Tilje Formations sandstone (Lower-Middle Jurassic)
fields_with_data.api_source: BP crude assay: Skarv blend 43.3 API at 60/60F, 0.205% sulfur, 0.8091 kg/l density (medium-light, low sulfur). Note Skarv blend is gas/condensate dominated. Field-edge sources also cite 48.2 API in newer assays.
fields_with_data.drive: Gas injection (to 2022), then gas blowdown; aquifer support; depletion in gas segments
fields_with_data.notes: Oil + gas + condensate field. Reservoir 3300-3700m. 3 oil segments (A, B, C), Idun is separate gas accumulation. Skarv blend assay: API 43.3, S 0.205%, density 0.8091 kg/l, viscosity 1.956 cst @ 40C, pour point -33C, wax 5.0%. Gas blowdown started B/C segments March 2022. Pressure falling 6 bar/month. ~80% of EUR oil produced.

fields_with_data.license: Multi-license area (PL442, PL272, PL035, PL035C, PL873, PL364)
fields_with_data.formation: Mixed: Middle Jurassic Hugin/Sleipner Fm sandstones (Hugin fields), Eocene Frigg Fm sandstones (Frigg Gamma Delta), Brent group Tarbert/Etive/Ness (Munin)
fields_with_data.api_source: Mix of oil + gas + condensate; Frigg Delta heavier than Gamma; Hugin/Sleipner sandstones host condensate. Crude expected medium API
fields_with_data.drive: Water injection (Frøy, Frigg Gamma Delta, Munin); depletion (Fulla, Ørn); aquifer support
fields_with_data.notes: Yggdrasil area: 10 discoveries over 60km trend. Aker BP 2P reserves 290 mmbbl oil + 63 mmboe NGL + 198 mmboe gas. Hub: PDQ platform Hugin A on Frigg Gamma Delta + Hugin B NUI on Frøy + Munin unmanned processing on Krafla. PDOs submitted Dec 2022.

fields_with_data.formation: Middle Jurassic Brent Group: Tarbert (upper/middle), Etive, Ness Formations (delta plain to marginal marine)
fields_with_data.api_source: Brent Group sandstones typically light/medium oil; mixed gas+oil+condensate; field reservoirs described as 'complex with large fluid variations'
fields_with_data.drive: Subsea templates, mixed depletion and water injection (21 producers + 3 injectors planned)
fields_with_data.notes: Aka Munin. 3 areas: Krafla (3 segments), Sentral (Beerenberg, Slemmestad, Haraldsplass), Askja (Vest, Øst, Sørøst, Madam Felle). Varying pressures, fluid phases (gas/oil) and contacts across compartments. Recoverable: 19.5 MSm3 oil + 22.2 GSm3 gas + 10.1 MSm3 NGL = 325 mmboe.

fields_with_data.formation: Middle Jurassic Brent Group Tarbert Formation (delta front, estuarine); Ness Formation
fields_with_data.api_source: Gas-condensate field per ASOR 2024 (lean gas-condensate); condensate API typically 45-55
fields_with_data.notes: Lean gas-condensate from Tarbert and Ness Formations. Includes Lille-Frigg redevelopment (originally produced 1994-1999, 2.3 GSm3 gas + 1.2 MSm3 condensate). Two slanted Fulla producers + 1 horizontal Lille-Frigg producer. Aker BP 2P: 8 mmbbl oil + 10 mmboe NGL + 53 mmboe gas.

fields_with_data.formation: Middle Jurassic Hugin and Sleipner Formations (fluvial to marginal marine sandstones with tidal influence)
fields_with_data.api_source: Brent area sandstones; original Elf development 1995-2001 produced 5.9 MSm3 oil + 1.7 GSm3 gas (suggests GOR ~290 m3/m3); medium-light oil
fields_with_data.notes: Redevelopment of historic Frøy field (Elf operated 1995-2001). Complex architecture, variable properties. 12-slot NUI (Hugin B) tied to Hugin A. 6 producers + 2 water injectors. Aker BP 2P: 31 mmbbl oil + 1 NGL + 7 gas.

fields_with_data.formation: Middle Jurassic Brent Group (Tarbert Formation, Etive/Ness) - same as Krafla
fields_with_data.api_source: Note: ASOR 2024 uses Munin = former Krafla naming. Reserves: 106 mmbbl oil + 40 NGL + 107 gas = 253 mmboe 2P
fields_with_data.notes: Munin = renamed Krafla. 11 oil/gas discoveries across Krafla, Sentral, Askja areas. Brent Group reservoirs with varying fluid types and contacts. Significant gas component. Subsea tieback with two-phase separation on platform, gas to Kårstø via Statpipe.

fields_with_data.formation: Late Palaeocene Heimdal Formation turbidite sandstone
fields_with_data.api_source: Heimdal area analog; bubble point ~60 bar, GOR ~40 Sm3/Sm3 (very low)
fields_with_data.drive: Water drive from Heimdal aquifer + downhole gas lift
fields_with_data.notes: DETAILED DATA from ASOR 2024: Reservoir pressure ~200 bar, temperature ~80C, bubble point ~60 bar, GOR ~40 Sm3/Sm3. Oil viscosity 0.9 cP Trell / 2.0 cP Trine. NTG >90%, porosity 26%, perm >1D. Three sub-discoveries: Trell, Trell North, Trine.

fields_with_data.formation: Late to middle-early Jurassic Draupne sandstones; Statfjord and Vestland Groups
fields_with_data.api_source: Johan Sverdrup blend is well-known medium crude ~28 API. ASOR 2024 confirms GOR 40-80 Sm3/Sm3 and 2 cP viscosity
fields_with_data.notes: DETAILED DATA from ASOR 2024: 'highly undersaturated oil with low GOR ranging 40-80 Sm3/Sm3, viscosity ~2 cP'. Apex at 1840m TVD MSL, FWLs 1922-1934m. Phase 1 + Phase 2 plateau 755,000 bbl/d (May 2023). CoP 2055.

fields_with_data.formation: Upper Jurassic Farsund Formation (turbidite) + Ula Formation (shallow marine sandstone)
fields_with_data.api_source: Gas-condensate field per ASOR 2024 - high pressure high temperature reservoir
fields_with_data.notes: DETAILED HPHT DATA from ASOR 2024: pressure 950-1050 bar, temperature 165-185C. Gas-condensate. Two Upper Jurassic reservoirs at ~5000m depth. 2/4-14 blowout (1989) lasted 7-11 months and depleted Farsund Fm. Tied via 50km pipeline to Valhall.

fields_with_data.formation: Middle Jurassic Garn Formation sandstone (moderate quality, overpressured)
fields_with_data.api_source: Gas/condensate discovery per ASOR 2024 - typical Garn Fm condensate ~50 API
fields_with_data.notes: Gas/condensate. Significant overpressure: 667 bar reservoir pressure (per ASOR 2024). 2 horizontal producers, 4-slot template tied to Skarv FPSO via new manifold/riser.

fields_with_data.formation: Middle Jurassic Garn Formation sandstone (very good quality)
fields_with_data.api_source: Gas/condensate per ASOR 2024
fields_with_data.notes: Gas/condensate. Over-pressured: 473 bar @ 3523m TVD MSL (per ASOR 2024).

fields_with_data.operator: Sval Energi / Aker BP from Feb 2025
fields_with_data.formation: Upper Jurassic Ula Formation sandstone (high quality)
fields_with_data.api_source: Same Ula Fm fluid system as Ula field. ASOR 2024 confirms 'high-quality, light crude oil'
fields_with_data.notes: DETAILED DATA from ASOR 2024: Initial pressure 409 bar @ 2917.5m TVDss, temperature 125C. 485m thick oil column of light crude. CoP 2026.

summary_observations: Aker BP Annual Statement of Reserves 2024 is the single most valuable source - rich qualitative reservoir descriptions and limited quantitative PVT data (GOR, bubble point, viscosity, pressure, temperature) for several fields, especially Tyrving, Johan Sverdrup, Fenris, Ørn, Idun Nord, Oda.

For the legacy producing fields (Edvard Grieg, Ivar Aasen, Alvheim, Volund, Vilje, Bøyla, Skogul, Valhall, Hod, Ula, Tambar, Skarv), ASOR provides formation/age, depth, drive mechanism, geological description but limited fluid PVT.
Best PVT data was found from external sources: Skarv (BP crude assay 43.3 API), Alvheim (Equinor assay ~33.9 API), Valhall (paraffinic 36 API), Ula (40 API), Hod (36 API, same as Valhall).

Yggdrasil area (pre-peak): Reserves quantified at 290 mmbbl oil + 63 NGL + 198 gas (2P). Mixed fluids - Brent Group (Munin), Hugin/Sleipner (Frøy, Langfjellet, Rind), Frigg (Gamma Delta), Tarbert/Ness (Fulla=condensate).
Fluid temperature data poor across the board except for Tyrving (80C), Edvard Grieg (~77C), Oda (125C), Fenris (165-185C).
GOR data sparse - Tyrving 40 Sm3/Sm3 (very low), Johan Sverdrup 40-80, Alvheim ~80 (from Alvheim assay), Frøy historic implied ~290 from cumulative ratio.
